description: This file controls the different functions for all missiles and their statistics. Missiles are projectiles used throughout the game for attacks, skills, and special effects. Any column field name starting with "*" is considered a comment field and is not used by the game.
source: Colonnes du .txt (BKVince 3.2) ; types/descriptions du guide TXT eezstreet-d2rdoc.

columns.name: Missile
columns.description: Defines the unique name ID for the missile, which is how other files can reference the missile. The order of defined missiles will determine their ID numbers, so they should not be reordered.

columns.name: pCltDoFunc
columns.description: Uses an ID value to select a function for the missile's behavior while active every frame on the client side. This is more about handling the local graphics while the missile is moving.

columns.name: pCltHitFunc
columns.description: Uses an ID value to select a specialized function for the missile's behavior when hitting something on the client side. This is more about handling the local graphics at the moment of missile collision.

columns.name: pSrvDoFunc
columns.description: Uses an ID value to select a specialized function for the missile's behavior while active every frame on the server side

columns.name: pSrvHitFunc
columns.description: Uses an ID value to select a specialized function for the missile's behavior when hitting something on the server side

columns.name: pSrvDmgFunc
columns.description: Uses an ID value to select a specialized function that gets called before damaging a unit on the server side

columns.name: SrvCalc1
columns.description: Numeric calculation field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDoFunc field. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: Param1
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDoFunc field.

columns.name: Param2
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDoFunc field.

columns.name: Param3
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDoFunc field.

columns.name: Param4
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDoFunc field.

columns.name: Param5
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDoFunc field.

columns.name: CltCalc1
columns.description: Numeric calculation field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltDoFunc field. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: CltParam1
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltDoFunc field.

columns.name: CltParam2
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltDoFunc field.

columns.name: CltParam3
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltDoFunc field.

columns.name: CltParam4
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltDoFunc field.

columns.name: CltParam5
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltDoFunc field.

columns.name: SHitCalc1
columns.description: Numeric calculation field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvHitFunc field. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: sHitPar1
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvHitFunc field.

columns.name: sHitPar2
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvHitFunc field.

columns.name: sHitPar3
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvHitFunc field.

columns.name: CHitCalc1
columns.description: Numeric calculation field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltHitFunc field. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: cHitPar1
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltHitFunc field.

columns.name: cHitPar2
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltHitFunc field.

columns.name: cHitPar3
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pCltHitFunc field.

columns.name: DmgCalc1
columns.description: Numeric calculation field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDmgFunc field. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: dParam1
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDmgFunc field.

columns.name: dParam2
columns.description: Integer field. Used as a parameter for the .pSrvDmgFunc field.

columns.name: Vel
columns.description: The baseline velocity of the missile, which is the speed at which the missile moves in the game world. This is measured by distance in pixels traveled per frame.

columns.name: MaxVel
columns.description: The maximum velocity of the missile. If the missile's current velocity increases (based on other fields), then this field controls how high the velocity is allowed to go.

columns.name: VelLev
columns.description: Adds extra velocity based on the caster unit's level. Each level gained beyond level 1 will add this value to the baseline .Vel field.

columns.name: Accel
columns.description: Controls the acceleration of the missile's movement. A positive value will increase the missile's velocity per frame. A negative value will decrease the missile's velocity per frame. The bigger positive or negative values will cause the velocity to change faster per frame.

columns.name: Range
columns.description: Controls the duration that the missile will exist for after it is created. This is measured in frames where 25 Frames = 1 second. Note that there are some instances where a skill function will overwrite this. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: Radius
columns.description: Specify the radius of the missile. This is different from its size. The radius is used for missile function searches and for vfx rendering. The size is used for collision and overlap checks. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: Light
columns.description: Controls the missile's Light Radius size (measured in grid sub-tiles)

columns.name: Flicker
columns.description: If greater than 0, then every 4th frame while the missile is active, the Light Radius will randomly change in size between base size to its base size plus this value (measured in grid sub-tiles)

columns.name: Red
columns.description: Controls the red color value of the missile's Light Radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: Green
columns.description: Controls the green color value of the monster's Light Radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: Blue
columns.description: Controls the blue color value of the monster's Light Radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: InitSteps
columns.description: The number of frames the missile needs to be alive until it becomes visible on the game client. If the missile's current duration in frame count is less than this value, then the missile will appear invisible.

columns.name: Activate
columns.description: The number of frames the missile needs to be alive until it becomes active. If the missile's current duration in frame count is less than this value, then the missile will not collide.

columns.name: LoopAnim
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile's animation will repeat once the previous animation finishes. If equals 0, then the missile's animation will only play once, which can cause the missile to appear invisible at the end of the animation, but it will still be alive.

columns.name: CelFile
columns.description: Defines which DCC missile file to use for the visual graphics of the missile

columns.name: animrate
columns.description: Controls the visual speed of the missile's animation graphics. The overall missile animation rate is calculated as the following: 256 * [.animrate] / 1024

columns.name: AnimLen
columns.description: Defines the length of the missile's animation in frames where 25 Frames = 1 second. This field can sometimes be used to calculate the missile animation rate, depending on the missile function used.

columns.name: AnimSpeed
columns.description: Controls the visual speed of the missile's animation graphics on the client side (Measured in 16ths, where 16 equals 1 frame per second). This can be overridden by certain missile functions.

columns.name: RandStart
columns.description: If this value is greater than 0, then the missile will start at a random frame between 0 and this value when it begins its animation.

columns.name: SubLoop
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will use a specific sequence of its animation while it is alive, depending on its creation. If equals 0, then the missile will not use a sequenced animation.

columns.name: SubStart
columns.description: The starting frame of the sequence animation. This requires that the .SubLoop field is enabled.

columns.name: SubStop
columns.description: The ending frame of the sequence animation. After reaching this frame, then the sequenced animation will loop back to the .SubStart frame. This requires that the .SubLoop field is enabled.

columns.name: CollideType
columns.description: Defines the missile's collision type, which controls what units, objects, or parts of the environment that the missile can impact

columns.name: CollideKill
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will be destroyed when it collides with something. If equals 0, then the missile will not be destroyed when it collides with something.

columns.name: CollideFriend
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile can collide with friendly units, including the caster. If equals 0, then the missile will ignore friendly units.

columns.name: LastCollide
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will track the last unit that it collided with, which is useful for making sure the missile does not hit the same unit twice. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: Collision
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will have a missile type path placement collision mask when it is initialized or moved. If equals 0, then the missile will have no placement collision mask when it is created or moved.

columns.name: ClientCol
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will check collision on the client, depending on the missile's .CollideType field. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: CollisionOverlap
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then an overlap collision check is done on the current missile location before stepping the missile on its next path location.

columns.name: ClientSend
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the server will create the missile on the client. This can be used when reloading area levels or transitioning units between areas. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: NextHit
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will use the next delay. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: NextDelay
columns.description: Controls the delay in frame length until this and any other missiles from the same skill cast are allowed to hit the same unit again. This field relies on the .NextHit field being enabled.

columns.name: xoffset
columns.description: Specifies the X, Y, and Z location coordinates (measured in pixels) to offset to visually draw the missile based on its actual location. This will only offset the visual graphics of the missile, not the missile itself. The Z axis controls the visual height of the missile.

columns.name: yoffset
columns.description: Specifies the X, Y, and Z location coordinates (measured in pixels) to offset to visually draw the missile based on its actual location. This will only offset the visual graphics of the missile, not the missile itself. The Z axis controls the visual height of the missile.

columns.name: zoffset
columns.description: Specifies the X, Y, and Z location coordinates (measured in pixels) to offset to visually draw the missile based on its actual location. This will only offset the visual graphics of the missile, not the missile itself. The Z axis controls the visual height of the missile.

columns.name: Size
columns.description: Defines the diameter in sub-tiles (for both the X and Y axis) that the missile will occupy. This affects how the missile will collide with something or how the game will handle placement for the missile.

columns.name: SrcTown
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will be destroyed if the caster unit is located in an act town. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: CltSrcTown
columns.description: If this value is greater than 0 and the .LoopAnim field is disabled, then this field will control which frame to set the missile's animation when the player is in town. This value gets subtracted from the .AnimLen value to determine the frame to set the missile's animation.

columns.name: CanDestroy
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile can be attacked and destroyed. If equals 0, then the missile cannot be attacked.

columns.name: ToHit
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then this missile will use the caster's Attack Rating stat to determine if the missile should hit its target. If equals 0, then the missile will always hit its target.

columns.name: AlwaysExplode
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will always process an explosion when it is killed, which can use a server hit function (See .pSrvHitFunc) and can use the .HitSound and .ExplosionMissile fields on the client side. If equals 0, then the missile will only rely on proper collision hits to process an explosion.

columns.name: Explosion
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will be classified as an explosion which will make it use different handlers for finding nearby units and dealing damage. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: Town
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile is allowed to be alive when in a town area. If equals 0, then the missile will be immediately destroyed when located within a town area.

columns.name: NoUniqueMod
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will not receive bonuses from Unique monster modifiers. If equals 0, then the missile will receive bonuses from Unique monster modifiers.

columns.name: NoMultiShot
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will not be affected by the Multi-Shot monster modifier. If equals 0, then the missile will be affected by the Multi-Shot monster modifier.

columns.name: Holy
columns.description: Controls a bit field flag where each value is a code to allow the missile to damage a certain type of monster. Leave blank to damage all units.

columns.name: CanSlow
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile can be affected by the "slowmissiles" state (See states). If equals 0, then the missile will ignore the "slowmissiles" state.

columns.name: ReturnFire
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then missile can trigger the Sorceress Chilling Armor event function. If equals 0, then this missile will not trigger that function.

columns.name: GetHit
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will cause the target unit to enter the Get Hit mode (GH), which acts as the hit recovery mode. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: SoftHit
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will cause a soft hit on the unit, which can trigger a blood splatter effect, hit flash, and/or a hit sound. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: KnockBack
columns.description: Controls the percentage chance (out of 100) that the target unit will be knocked back when hit by the missile

columns.name: Trans
columns.description: Controls the alpha mode for how the missile is displayed, which can affect transparency and blending

columns.name: Pierce
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then allow the Pierce modifier function to work with this missile. If equals 0, then do not allow Pierce to work with this missile.

columns.name: MissileSkill
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile will look up the skill that created it and use that skill's damage instead of the missile damage. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: Skill
columns.description: This will look up the specified skill's damage and use it for the missile instead of using the missile's defined damage.

columns.name: ResultFlags
columns.description: Controls different flags that can affect how the target reacts after being hit by the missile. Uses an integer value to check against different bit fields by using the "&" operator. For example, if the value equals 5 (binary = 101) then that returns true for both the 4 (binary = 100) and 1 (binary = 1) bit field values.

columns.name: HitFlags
columns.description: Controls different flags that can affect the damage dealt when the target is hit by the missile. Uses an integer value to check against different bit fields by using the "&" operator. For example, if the value equals 6 (binary = 110) then that returns true for both the 4 (binary = 100) and 2 (binary = 10) bit field values.

columns.name: HitShift
columns.description: Controls the percentage modifier for the missile's damage. This value cannot be less than 0 or greater than 8. This is calculated in 256ths, where 8=256/256, 7=128/256, 6=64/256, 5=32/256, 4=16/256, 3=8/256, 2=4/256, 1=2/256, and 0=1/256.

columns.name: ApplyMastery
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then apply the caster's elemental mastery bonus modifiers to the missile's elemental damage. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: SrcDamage
columns.description: Controls how much of the source unit's damage should be added to the missile's damage. This is calculated in 128ths and acts as a percentage modifier for the source unit's damage that added to the missile. If equals -1 or 0, then the source damage is not included.

columns.name: Half2HSrc
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1 and the source unit is currently wielding a 2-Handed weapon, then the source damage (see .SrcDamage) is reduced by 50%. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: SrcMissDmg
columns.description: If the missile was created by another missile, then this controls how much of the source missile's damage should be added to this missile's damage. This is calculated in 128ths and acts as a percentage modifier for the source missile's damage that added to this missile. If equals 0, then the source damage is not included.

columns.name: MinDamage
columns.description: Minimum baseline physical damage dealt by the missile

columns.name: MinLevDam1
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MinLevDam2
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MinLevDam3
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MinLevDam4
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MinLevDam5
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxDamage
columns.description: Maximum baseline physical damage dealt by the missile

columns.name: MaxLevDam1
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxLevDam2
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxLevDam3
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxLevDam4
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxLevDam5
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum physical damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: DmgSymPerCalc
columns.description: Calculation Field. Determines the percentage increase to the physical damage dealt by the missile based on specified skill levels. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: EType
columns.description: Defines the type of elemental damage dealt by the missile. If this field is empty, then the related elemental fields below will not be used. Any type that has length uses the .ELen field.

columns.name: EMin
columns.description: Minimum baseline elemental damage dealt by the missile

columns.name: MinELev1
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field number.

columns.name: MinELev2
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field number.

columns.name: MinELev3
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field number.

columns.name: MinELev4
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field number.

columns.name: MinELev5
columns.description: Controls the additional minimum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field number.

columns.name: EMax
columns.description: Maximum baseline elemental damage dealt by the missile

columns.name: MaxELev1
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxELev2
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxELev3
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxELev4
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: MaxELev5
columns.description: Controls the additional maximum elemental damage dealt by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 5 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17-22, 23-28, 29 and beyond. These 5 level thresholds correlate to each field.

columns.name: EDmgSymPerCalc
columns.description: Calculation Field. Determines the percentage increase to the elemental damage dealt by the missile based on specified skill levels. Missile scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: ELen
columns.description: The baseline elemental duration dealt by the missile. This is calculated in frame lengths where 25 Frames = 1 second. These fields only apply to appropriate elemental types with a duration.

columns.name: ELevLen1
columns.description: Controls the additional elemental duration added by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 3 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17 and beyond. These 3 level thresholds correlate to each field. These fields only apply to appropriate elemental types with a duration.

columns.name: ELevLen2
columns.description: Controls the additional elemental duration added by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 3 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17 and beyond. These 3 level thresholds correlate to each field. These fields only apply to appropriate elemental types with a duration.

columns.name: ELevLen3
columns.description: Controls the additional elemental duration added by the missile, calculated using the leveling formula between 3 level thresholds of the missile's current level. The level thresholds are levels 2-8, 9-16, 17 and beyond. These 3 level thresholds correlate to each field. These fields only apply to appropriate elemental types with a duration.

columns.name: HitClass
columns.description: Defines the missil's own hit class into the damage routines, mainly used for determining hit sound effects and overlays. This field only handles the hit class layers, so values beyond these defined bits are ignored. Uses an integer value to check against different bit fields by using the "&" operator. For example, if the value equals 6 (binary = 110) then that returns true for both the 4 (binary = 100) and 2 (binary = 10) bit field values.

columns.name: NumDirections
columns.description: The number of directions allowed by the missile, based on the DCC file used (see .CelFile). This value should be within the power of 2, with a minimum value of 1 or up to a maximum value of 64.

columns.name: LocalBlood
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then change the color of blood missiles to green. If equals 0, then keep the blood missiles colored the default red.

columns.name: DamageRate
columns.description: Controls the "damage_framerate" stat (Calculated in 1024ths), which acts as a percentage multiplier for the physical damage reduction and magic damage reduction stat modifiers, when performing damage resistance calculations. This is only enabled if the value is greater than 0.

columns.name: TravelSound
columns.description: Used when the missile is created and while it is alive.

columns.name: HitSound
columns.description: Used when the collides with a target.

columns.name: OnDiedSound
columns.description: Optional sound to play when the missile dies.

columns.name: ProgSound
columns.description: Used for a programmed special event based on the client function.

columns.name: ProgOverlay
columns.description: Used for a programmed special event based on the server or client function.

columns.name: ExplosionMissile
columns.description: Used for the missile created on the client when this missile explodes.

columns.name: SubMissile1
columns.description: Used for creating a new missile based on the server function used.

columns.name: SubMissile2
columns.description: Used for creating a new missile based on the server function used.

columns.name: SubMissile3
columns.description: Used for creating a new missile based on the server function used.

columns.name: HitSubMissile1
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the server function used.

columns.name: HitSubMissile2
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the server function used.

columns.name: HitSubMissile3
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the server function used.

columns.name: HitSubMissile4
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the server function used.

columns.name: CltSubMissile1
columns.description: Used for creating a new missile based on the client function used.

columns.name: CltSubMissile2
columns.description: Used for creating a new missile based on the client function used.

columns.name: CltSubMissile3
columns.description: Used for creating a new missile based on the client function used.

columns.name: CltHitSubMissile1
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the client function used.

columns.name: CltHitSubMissile2
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the client function used.

columns.name: CltHitSubMissile3
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the client function used.

columns.name: CltHitSubMissile4
columns.description: Used for a new missile after a collision, based on the client function used.

columns.name: MissileWeaponVFX
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the missile is being represented by a weapon or a vfx based on a weapon.
